     Edit the Claude source, not this file — regenerate instead. -->

> **Plane MCP pre-flight (Copilot — check this before the Plane path steps below):**
>
> Scan your available tool list for at least one tool matching `mcp__plane__*`, `plane_*`, or `mcp_plane_*`.
> - **None found → STOP.** Print: "Error: Plane MCP tools are not accessible. In VS Code, open the MCP panel (View → MCP Servers), ensure the `plane` server shows as Connected, and retry. Do not fall back to creating markdown tickets." Do not continue to the Markdown path.
> - **Found → note the active prefix** (e.g. `mcp__plane__` or `plane_`) and use it for every tool call in this session.
>
> **If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists, the Markdown path is off-limits** regardless of MCP status — never silently degrade to local TKT-*.md files when the project uses Plane.


# Show a Ticket's Lifecycle Timeline

Render a human-readable timeline of everything that's happened to a ticket. Useful when coming back to a project after time away and needing to remember what state things are in.

## Pre-flight: detect backend

- If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists → **Plane path**.
- Else if `.claude/ticket-config.md` + `tickets/` → **Markdown path**.
- Else: error, tell the user to run `/ticket-install`.

## Input (both backends)

Argument: `{ID}` (e.g. `SMOKE-15` / `TKT-005`), or a bare number (resolves per project identifier + padding rules as in `/ticket-new`).

If no argument is given: render a one-line summary of every ticket in the active set.

`/ticket-status` is **read-only** and works on terminal tickets too (no `/ticket-reopen` gate).


## Plane path

### Steps

0. **Auto-reap preflight (silent).** Run `/ticket-cleanup` no-arg logic inline (execution-layer, unchanged). One-line note if anything reaped; silent otherwise.

1. **Load config.** Read `.claude/plane-config.md` for project ID, project identifier, state ID → name map, and label ID → name map.

2. **Fetch — two paths:**
   - **With `{ID}`** (single-ticket lifecycle view): `mcp__plane__retrieve_work_item_by_identifier(project_identifier, sequence_id)` — 404 → error. This path stays as lifecycle reconstruction; the agent-authored timeline is richer than Plane's raw activity log and is worth the one MCP call.
   - **No argument** (active-set overview): **URL handoff — no MCP fetch.** Read `.claude/plane-config.md`'s `## View URLs` section and print the `Active` URL as a clickable markdown link:
     ```
     Open: [Active tickets in {IDENT}]({url})
     ```
     Then stop. Plane's UI groups by state, sorts by priority, and shows everything an agent-rendered table would have. No summary line, no state breakdown in text.

     **If `## View URLs` is absent** (pre-view-URLs install): compose the Active URL inline — don't go spec-spelunking. Template: `{PLANE_BASE_URL}/{workspace-slug}/projects/{project-uuid}/issues/?state_group=backlog,unstarted,started&order_by=-priority`. Source for each piece:
     - `workspace-slug` → `- Workspace slug:` line of `plane-config.md`. If that value is the literal placeholder `(inferred from Plane MCP server env vars; no need to hard-code)`, fall back to `~/.claude.json` → `mcpServers.plane.env.PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG`; final fallback `AskUserQuestion("workspace slug? e.g. throughline")`. Write the resolved value back into `plane-config.md`'s header so it's cached.
     - `PLANE_BASE_URL` → `~/.claude.json` → `mcpServers.plane.env.PLANE_BASE_URL`; fallback `AskUserQuestion`.
     - `project-uuid` → from `- Project ID:` line of `plane-config.md`.

     Append the `## View URLs` section to `plane-config.md` (with the Active URL at minimum — other views can be lazy-filled later by `/ticket-list`/`/ticket-promote` when they run). Then print the Active link and stop.

3. **Single-ticket view** (only when `{ID}` provided): also fetch the activity log (`mcp__plane__list_work_item_activities`) and the comments (`mcp__plane__list_work_item_comments`). Parse markers from comments: `[investigated_at: <sha>]`, `[delegated_to: <agent>]`, `[original_id: <TKT-NNN>]`.

4. **Reconstruct the lifecycle** (single-ticket path only) from state history (activity log `state` field changes) + comment markers + description sections (look for `<h3>Investigation</h3>`, `<h3>Implementation Plan</h3>`, `<h3>Files Changed</h3>`, `<h3>Automated Checks</h3>`, `<h3>Verification Checklist (for human)</h3>`):

   | Signal | Lifecycle event |
   |---|---|
   | Work item created (`verb: created`) | created (Backlog) |
   | Description contains `<h3>Investigation</h3>` OR a `[investigated_at]` comment marker | investigated |
   | State changed to Ready | investigation accepted |
   | State changed to In Progress | approved / implementing |
   | Description contains `<h3>Files Changed</h3>` | implementation done |
   | State changed to In Review | ready for review |
   | Description contains `<h3>Automated Checks</h3>` + `<h3>Verification Checklist</h3>` | reviewed (checklist generated) |
   | State changed to Done | shipped |
   | State changed to Cancelled | closed (deferred or wontfix — check the cancel comment's `deferred:` / `wontfix:` prefix) |
   | `[delegated_to: <agent>]` comment with no later clear-delegated comment | currently delegated (agent named) |

5. **Render the single-ticket view:**
   ```
   {ID}: {title}
     State: {state}      Priority: {priority}      Labels: {comma-separated}
     Created: {date}     Updated: {date}

     Lifecycle:
       ✓ created                                                {date}
       ✓ investigated  (sha: {short})                           {date}
       ✓ approved  → branch ticket/{lowercased-id}-{slug}       {date}
       ✓ implemented                                            {date}
       → in review                                              {date}
       ⋯ ship pending

     Next action:
       Run /ticket-ship {ID}
   ```

   (The active-set view is a URL handoff — step 2, no-argument branch — not rendered here.)

### Rules (Plane path)

- Read-only: no state changes, no description edits, no comments.
- Always show the "Next action" line — it's the most important signal for someone returning cold.
- If Plane's activity log is sparse (e.g., long history got rolled up), fall back to description-section presence + comment markers for lifecycle reconstruction.
- Be terse. Reorientation, not storytelling.


## Markdown path

*(Unchanged. Kept in-tree until Plan 3 completes.)*

### Steps

0. **Auto-reap preflight (silent):** run the `/ticket-cleanup` no-arg logic inline — reap any stale worktrees (tickets in terminal folders or missing). If anything was reaped, print a one-line note at the top ("auto-reaped N stale worktrees"). If nothing was reaped, say nothing.

1. **Read `.claude/ticket-config.md`** to find the tickets directory.

2. **Read the ticket file**. Parse:
   - Frontmatter: id, title, type, status, priority, branch, created, updated
   - Delegation Log section (if present)
   - Which sections have content (Investigation, Proposed Solution, Implementation Plan, Files Changed, Test Report, Verification Checklist, Peer Review *)

3. **Reconstruct the lifecycle** from these signals. A ticket's history is:
   - Created → status was `open`
   - Investigation filled → an investigate phase ran (Claude Code locally OR delegated)
   - Status went `proposed` → investigation accepted
   - Branch field set → either `/ticket-approve` or `/ticket-delegate ... implement` ran
   - Files Changed filled → implementation done
   - Verification Checklist filled → review done
   - Status `shipped` → ticket shipped

   Use the Delegation Log entries (if present) to attribute phases to specific tools/models/timestamps.

4. **Render the timeline** as a checklist with timestamps and attributions where known:

   ```
   TKT-005: Redesign project picker dropdown
     Status: delegated      Branch: ticket/tkt-005-redesign-project-picker
     Type: enhancement      Priority: medium
     Created: 2026-04-05    Updated: 2026-04-07

     Lifecycle:
       ✓ created                                                2026-04-05
       ✓ investigated (Claude Code)                             2026-04-05
       ✓ verified-investigate (Gemini via Copilot)              2026-04-06
           → 3 issues raised, all addressed in v2
       ✓ investigated v2 (Claude Code, revised)                 2026-04-06
       ✓ approved → branch created                              2026-04-06
       → delegated (implement) — awaiting Gemini                2026-04-07
       ⋯ collect pending
       ⋯ review pending
       ⋯ ship pending

     Next action:
       Run /run-brief in Copilot Chat (with Gemini) on:
         tickets/TKT-005.implement.brief.md
       Then come back and run: /ticket-collect TKT-005
   ```

5. **For the active-set view** (no argument), render a one-line-per-ticket summary instead:

   ```
   Active tickets in this project:
     TKT-003  delegated     verify-implement (Gemini reviewing)
     TKT-005  delegated     implement (Gemini implementing)
     TKT-007  proposed      ready for /ticket-approve
     TKT-008  open          ready for /ticket-investigate

   Run /ticket-status {ID} for the full timeline of any ticket.
   ```

### Rules (markdown path)

- This command is read-only. It does not modify the ticket file or any other state.
- If the ticket has no Delegation Log, infer phases from which sections are filled in (best-effort timeline without timestamps).
- Always show the "Next action" line — it's the most important part for someone returning cold.
- Be terse. The point is to reorient quickly, not to read paragraphs.

## Compatibility Notes

- **MCP tool mapping:** In VS Code Copilot, Plane MCP tools are typically exposed as `plane_<tool_name>` (e.g. `plane_create_work_item`), not `mcp__plane__<tool_name>` (Claude Code convention). The Plane MCP pre-flight above detects which prefix is active and uses it for the session. For explicit IDs, prefer direct identifier lookup; if unavailable, use bounded project-scoped lookup + exact match.
- **AskUserQuestion:** Copilot has no equivalent structured-question tool. When the Claude source says "use AskUserQuestion", ask the user in chat with a numbered or bulleted choice and wait for a reply.
- **Parallel subagents → sequential loop:** if the Claude source fans out subagents in parallel (e.g. ticket-investigate multi-ticket, ticket-batch), run them as a sequential loop in one agent session instead. Output and behavior are identical; only wall-clock differs.
- **Backend dispatch:** the "Pre-flight: detect backend" block is authoritative in both hosts. `.claude/plane-config.md` presence routes to the Plane path; otherwise the Markdown path runs.
